output.queryType: Pre-build validation + risk assessment + immediate decision framework

extractedContext.coreProblem: HR teams spend excessive time manually screening resumes; need faster, consistent candidate ranking
extractedContext.targetMarket: Mid-size companies (50-500 employees), likely in tech/growth sectors
extractedContext.solution: AI-powered resume screening tool integrated with job descriptions
statedConcerns: Competition from established ATS platforms (Greenhouse, Lever) adding AI features
Trust and adoption risk - HR teams may not trust AI-only tool for hiring decisions
Sensitivity of use case (hiring decisions have legal/bias implications)
openDecisions: Go-to-market strategy (direct sales vs. self-serve vs. integration partnership)
Positioning (standalone tool vs. ATS add-on vs. workflow integration)
constraints: No mention of domain expertise in HR/recruitment

keyQuestions: What is your actual defensible differentiation vs. Greenhouse/Lever/iCIMS adding AI screening, and is it sustainable for 18+ months?
How will you acquire initial customers without a sales team, and what is the realistic CAC vs. $500 LTV math?
Should you build standalone or position as an integration/plugin to existing ATS platforms to reduce adoption friction?
What are the legal/bias risks of AI-only hiring decisions, and how will you mitigate liability concerns in your GTM?
What is your actual MVP scope to validate product-market fit in 3 months, and what customer validation should happen before building?

triage.rawText:
```json
{
  "ideaType": "B2B SaaS - HR Tech / Recruitment",
  "queryType": "Pre-build validation + risk assessment + immediate decision framework",
  "founderProfile": {
    "teamSize": 1,
    "technicalAbility": "strong",
    "budget": 15000,
    "experience": "5 years backend engineering",
    "timeline": "3 months to MVP"
  },
  "extractedContext": {
    "coreProblem": "HR teams spend excessive time manually screening resumes; need faster, consistent candidate ranking",
    "targetMarket": "Mid-size companies (50-500 employees), likely in tech/growth sectors",
    "solution": "AI-powered resume screening tool integrated with job descriptions",
    "revenueModel": "$500/month per company SaaS",
    "statedConcerns": [
      "Competition from established ATS platforms (Greenhouse, Lever) adding AI features",
      "Trust and adoption risk - HR teams may not trust AI-only tool for hiring decisions",
      "Sensitivity of use case (hiring decisions have legal/bias implications)"
    ],
    "openDecisions": [
      "Go-to-market strategy (direct sales vs. self-serve vs. integration partnership)",
      "Positioning (standalone tool vs. ATS add-on vs. workflow integration)",
      "MVP scope and feature set",
      "Customer acquisition and validation approach"
    ],
    "constraints": [
      "Solo founder with limited capital",
      "3-month timeline pressure",
      "No mention of domain expertise in HR/recruitment"
    ]
  },
  "sectionPriorities": {
    "marketAnalysis": "detailed",
    "competitivePosition": "detailed",
    "businessModel": "detailed",
    "technicalFeasibility": "standard",
    "decisionAgenda": "detailed",
    "riskMitigation": "detailed"
  },
  "keyQuestions": [
    "What is your actual defensible differentiation vs. Greenhouse/Lever/iCIMS adding AI screening, and is it sustainable for 18+ months?",
    "How will you acquire initial customers without a sales team, and what is the realistic CAC vs. $500 LTV math?",
    "Should you build standalone or position as an integration/plugin to existing ATS platforms to reduce adoption friction?",
    "What are the legal/bias risks of AI-only hiring decisions, and how will you mitigate liability concerns in your GTM?",
    "What is your actual MVP scope to validate product-market fit in 3 months, and what customer validation should happen before building?"
  ],
  "language": "en"
}
```

executiveSummary.summary: This is a crowded space where incumbents are already adding AI features and have distribution advantages. Your biggest challenge isn't technical—it's customer acquisition without HR domain expertise or existing relationships. The $500/month price point creates a difficult middle ground: too expensive for self-serve, too cheap for enterprise sales. Consider pivoting to an integration-first approach or finding a specific underserved niche.

marketSize.tam: $24B global recruitment software market (Grand View Research, 2023)
marketSize.som: $50M realistic addressable market for standalone AI screening tools
marketSize.methodology: Based on ~25K mid-size companies in target segment, 20% adoption ceiling, $500/month pricing

competitors.name: Greenhouse
competitors.description: Leading ATS with built-in AI screening features launched 2023
competitors.strength: Existing customer base, integrated workflow, enterprise trust
competitors.weakness: Complex for smaller teams, expensive implementation

competitors.name: Lever
competitors.description: Modern ATS with AI-powered candidate matching

competitors.name: HireVue
competitors.description: AI-powered video interviewing and assessment platform
competitors.weakness: Focused on video assessment, not resume screening

competitors.name: Workable
competitors.description: SMB-focused ATS with basic AI features
competitors.strength: Strong SMB market presence, affordable pricing
competitors.weakness: Limited AI sophistication compared to enterprise players

marketAnalysis.positioning: Position as ATS-agnostic screening layer that integrates with existing workflows rather than replacement
marketAnalysis.timing: Post-ChatGPT AI adoption is high, but incumbents are rapidly adding features. Window is closing.
gaps: Integration-first tools for existing ATS users

valueProposition.coreProblem: HR teams at growing companies spend 40+ hours per week manually screening resumes, creating bottlenecks in hiring
valueProposition.targetCustomer: HR managers at 100-300 employee tech/growth companies using basic ATS tools without advanced AI
valueProposition.uniqueAngle: ATS-agnostic screening that works with existing tools rather than forcing platform migration
valueProposition.whyNow: Remote work explosion increased application volumes 300%, making manual screening unsustainable

businessModel.revenueStrategy: Monthly SaaS subscription with usage-based pricing tiers
businessModel.pricingFramework: $200 base + $1 per resume screened (caps at $500/month) - aligns cost with value

unitEconomics.estimatedCAC: $2,000-3,000 (outbound sales required at this price point)

businessModel.scalabilityAssessment: Good gross margins (80%+) but high sales costs. Need product-led growth elements to scale efficiently.

technicalFeasibility.recommendedStack: Python/FastAPI backend, React frontend, OpenAI/Claude API, PostgreSQL

buildVsBuy.rationale: Use existing APIs like Affinda or Sovren - parsing is commodity
Core differentiation, need custom prompts and fine-tuning
Simple CRUD app, within your skillset

risks.description: Incumbents like Greenhouse already offer AI screening as part of integrated platforms
risks.mitigation: Focus on integration strategy - become the AI layer for existing ATS users

risks.description: No HR domain expertise or existing relationships for customer development
risks.mitigation: Partner with HR consultants or find co-founder with domain expertise

risks.description: AI hiring tools face increasing legal scrutiny for bias and discrimination
risks.mitigation: Build audit trails, bias testing, and compliance documentation from day one

risks.description: High CAC with limited runway means very few shots at product-market fit
risks.mitigation: Start with free/freemium tier to reduce acquisition costs

risks.description: AI API costs could eat margins at scale without careful optimization
risks.mitigation: Build cost monitoring and caching from MVP stage

week1to4: Interview 15 HR managers at target companies about current screening pain points
Audit 3 major ATS platforms' AI features to understand competitive gaps
Build basic resume parsing prototype using existing APIs
week5to8: Create integration mockups for top 3 ATS platforms
Test AI scoring accuracy with real job descriptions and resumes
week9to12: Build MVP with one ATS integration
Measure screening accuracy vs manual baseline

decisionAgenda.question: Standalone product vs ATS integration strategy?

options.tradeoff: Higher revenue potential vs much harder customer acquisition
Easier adoption vs lower revenue per customer

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Start with integration approach - reduces switching costs and leverages existing workflows

decisionAgenda.question: Customer acquisition strategy with limited budget?

options.tradeoff: Higher conversion vs unsustainable with $15K budget
Lower CAC vs longer sales cycles

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Freemium with partner channel - you can't afford traditional B2B sales

decisionAgenda.question: Which ATS platform to integrate with first?
decisionAgenda.recommendation: Start with Workable - less sophisticated AI, more open to partnerships

decisionAgenda.question: MVP scope for 3-month timeline?

options.tradeoff: Complete product vs longer time to market
Faster launch vs limited user experience

decisionAgenda.recommendation: API-first with basic dashboard - focus on proving AI accuracy before UX polish
